cowardice_flags.what_dodged: Malaysia's refusal to submit to the ICJ
cowardice_flags.what_honesty_requires: Malaysia's refusal is strategic dominance: they hold effective control, international recognition, and self-determination precedent. Submitting would legitimise a dead 148-year-old claim. The analysis hedges by calling it 'won't submit' without admitting this is wisdom, not cowardice.

cowardice_flags.what_dodged: Malaysia's 50 years of annual payments to Sulu heirs (1963-2013)
cowardice_flags.what_honesty_requires: Malaysia paid 'cession money' knowing it was political pragmatism to keep the peace with Sulu elites, not an admission the 1878 deed was a lease. Stopping payments after Lahad Datu proved the claim was always conditional on good behaviour. The analysis buries this.

It was a British-Malayan rubber-stamp exercise, not a referendum or genuine self-determination. No secret ballot, limited consultation, Cold War anti-communist framing. Self-determination was colonial convenience, yet it still binds under modern international law.

cowardice_flags.what_dodged: Philippines' claim as domestic political survival
cowardice_flags.what_honesty_requires: The claim is 99% Manila elite nationalism and Mindanao vote-bank theatre. No Filipino fisherman in Sulu cares about Sabah sovereignty. RA 5446 is kept alive because repealing it would be political suicide for any president.

false_balance.claim: The 'ceded vs leased' question remains legally unresolved
false_balance.evidence_direction: International law, effective control since 1881, 1963 Malaysia Agreement, UN recognition, and repeated court dismissals have settled it. Ancient ambiguous deeds do not override self-determination and uti possidetis juris.
false_balance.verdict: False balance. The analysis equates a dead colonial lease claim with Malaysia's living sovereignty.

false_balance.claim: Malaysia has won every court case but only on procedure, never on sovereignty merits
false_balance.evidence_direction: The Sulu arbitration was a jurisdiction farce. Malaysia's position has never been tested in ICJ precisely because it is overwhelmingly stronger on merits.
false_balance.verdict: Performative false equivalence. One side has territory, recognition, and self-determination; the other has a 148-year-old receipt.

false_balance.claim: Neither side can close the dispute
false_balance.evidence_direction: Malaysia can close it tomorrow by ignoring the claim; the world already does. Philippines cannot repeal RA 5446 because of domestic politics, not legal impossibility.
false_balance.verdict: False balance. Malaysia holds all cards; Philippines holds nostalgia.

audience_check: A Sulu-origin Sabahan fisherman in Semporna would roll his eyes — the analysis erases his Malaysian identity and his daily reality of stateless relatives. A KK taxi driver would laugh: 'ICJ? Just make the Filipinos stop sending boats.' A Manila taxi driver would shrug — 'Sabah? Nice, but my problem is traffic and inflation.' A Filipino academic would find it patronisingly 'balanced' while ignoring how the claim is pure elite nationalism. Anwar Ibrahim would find zero discomfort; the analysis is too polite about Project IC and the payments he himself continued.

hard_truths_missing.about: The 800,000+ Sulu-origin and Filipino population in Sabah
hard_truths_missing.truth: Cheap labour for palm oil and fisheries, demographic time-bomb, source of crime and security headaches. Most are integrated Sabahans who reject Philippine sovereignty but suffer because KL treats them as political pawns.

Real demographic engineering by Mahathir-era UMNO to flip Sabah Muslim-majority for votes. Royal Commission confirmed the mechanics. It directly created the stateless/underclass problem that fuels the claim.
Paying rent every year until 2013 was pragmatic rent-seeking, not sovereignty admission. Stopping it proved the payment was always a political leash.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Makes Malaysia look cynical instead of principled.

hard_truths_missing.truth: Pure leverage on migration deals, South China Sea coordination, and ASEAN horse-trading. Marcos Jr. revives it whenever oil or energy talks heat up.
hard_truths_missing.why_avoided: Calling it cynical realpolitik ruins polite diplomatic tone.

class_truths_avoided.truth: Manila and KL elites play sovereignty chess while Sulu fishermen and Sabah plantation workers pay in blood, statelessness, and poverty. The claim survives because poor border people have zero voice; rich politicians get nationalist applause.
class_truths_avoided.why_avoided: Analysis stays in safe diplomatic/legal language.

predictions.if_nothing_changes: Philippines will keep issuing notes and maps every election cycle; Malaysia will keep arresting undocumented migrants and ignoring the claim.
predictions.who_can_fix: Only a grand bargain: migration treaty, joint economic zone, citizenship pathway for long-term Sulu residents; Philippines repeals RA 5446.
predictions.why_they_wont: Philippine nationalism makes repeal career suicide; Malaysian politicians fear being called weak on sovereignty.

strongest_card.why: Lahad Datu 2013 is the only part that actually matters on the ground — real guns, real deaths, real security.

weakest_card.why: The 'ceded vs leased' ambiguity is legally irrelevant theatre in 2026. Self-determination and 63 years of effective control settled it. Pretending otherwise is the analysis's biggest hedge.

overall_assessment: This analysis is diplomatic cowardice dressed as nuance. It pretends two sovereign equals are locked in honourable deadlock when one side owns the land, the people, the recognition, and the guns, while the other clings to a 148-year receipt and domestic vote fodder. The only honest takeaway is that the claim is a zombie kept alive by elite nationalism on both sides while the real victims — stateless Sulu fishermen, exploited Sabah migrants, and border communities — get screwed.
